key_insight: Si-doping and higher Al content together trigger coordination loss and vacancy-mediated cation rearrangement that drives local beta-to-gamma phase transformation in (AlxGa1-x)2O3.
engineering_relevance: The result identifies a nanoscale structural warning signal for reliability risk in ultrawide-bandgap oxide devices and helps guide composition/doping choices to suppress phase instability.
method: Coordination-sensitive atom probe tomography to map local chemistry and coordination changes
Nearest-neighbor and radial distribution analyses to quantify reduced Ga first-shell coordination
Correlative STEM and DFT to validate phase-inclusion locations and the vacancy-mediated mechanism
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gamma-phase inclusions only at 6–17% Al with heavy Si doping (10^17–10^20 cm^-3), alongside lateral Al/Ga inhomogeneity and reduced Ga coordination that matches STEM-observed inclusions.
watch_item: The coordination-loss signature is powerful but must be interpreted carefully because it depends on local reconstruction quality and correlative alignment between atom probe and microscopy data.
paper_title: Coordination-Sensitive Nanoscale Analysis of Defect-Driven Phase Transformation in Si-Doped (AlXGa1-X)2O3
